segments.text: [Music]
in this video I'll show you how to map
your or into sonar Cube using the Azure
develops integration found within sonar
Cube there will be four parts to this
video the integration setup the user
authentication project onboarding as
well as the pool request analysis and
decoration after logging in you land on
the main Project screen where you can
create a project select more go to Azure
devops and this is where you can enter
your configuration you'll be able to do
the exact same thing under
Administration under the devops platform
integration section there's a aure
devops section Tab and click on create
configuration this will be the same
model that will drop down so let's head
back over to the previous place where we
wanted to enter our configuration over
here give this config configuration a
meaningful name and this will be what
your whole configuration will saved as a
URL also over here as you can see that I
took from my Azure devops instance and
as well as a personal access token click
on user settings in Azure develops and
go to personal access tokens where
you'll be able to generate a new access
token click on new token give it a name
and this token will be used for all
project integration if using the UI I'm
going to set for 90
days take note that you have to give it
the code read and write permissions so
over here I'm going to select the read
and write permissions and then create
once I press create uh token generated
I'm going to copy this and paste it
inside my personal access token field
press save configuration and this whole
configuration will save paste the same
token in and press list repositories and
you'll be able to see your aure devops
repositories loaded next we'll cover the
authentication options available which
will be saml and LP for the saml
authentication flow you can go under
Administration authentication and then
the samle section over here and this is
where you can authenticate users into
Sona Cube using the samle protocol since
Azure ad also supports saml you can then
connect azure ad and Sona Cube together
to authenticate users into Sona Cube you
can follow the step-by-step guide
available in our
documentation that shows you how to do
exactly that in addition you can also
integrate keycloak as well as OCTA via
saml to authenticate your users into
sonar cube next up is alep you can
actually use LP and configure this in
your sonar . properties File 2 to
connect your alep servers to your son
instance and this allows you to then use
the users inside your alab servers to
connect and to
authenticate into Sona Cube we allow
group synchronization and more than that
you can use this piece of documentation
to configure your sonar Cube instance
with your alep server or alep servers as
you can see over here you're able to use
multiple servers and note that
authentication will be tried on each
server in the order that they are listed
in the configuration until one succeeds
we'll move on now to talk about project
creation options there are three options
available the first of which would be to
create a new project via the sonac cube
UI guidance next you can use the sonac
cube API to programmatically create
projects lastly you're also able to
automatically create a project via the
first analysis by defining the sonar.
project key inside your son- project.
properties file or by defining it as an
option when running your Sona scanner
CLI command once you come into the Sona
Cube main project page click on create
project and then the Azure devops button
however before that take note that you
have to go over to Administration and
then General scroll all the way down
until you find the server based URL over
here set the base URL to your url of
your sonar Cube instance without the
slash at the back as you can see I've
already set it head back to your
projects and you can safely create and
onboard your Azure develops project onto
son Cube since you've already connected
your Azure devops instance to your Sona
Cube instance earlier on you'll be able
to then see your projects as well as the
repositories inside each project in this
case I'm going to connect the note JS
project that you see on my left and over
here I can select my new code setting I
can use the global setting or I can
specify the previous version number of
days or reference Branch to be used but
in this case I'm going to specify the
global
setting click on with Azure pipelines in
this case since I'll be using Azure
pipeline
and install the sonar Cube extension by
clicking on the link press on Get It
Free and this should install your sonar
Cube extension into your Azure devops
organization take note that this adds
sonar related functionality to your
Azure devops instance in this case you
can see that I've already installed it
over here press continue and then it
says that you have to go to Project
settings service connection add a
service connection of Type S Cube on the
left you can see that I'm going to to
the service connections create service
connection and I'm going to search up
son Cube over here I'm going to paste in
the server URL note that I've set this
earlier on in my Administration pane
this is why that server URL was able to
appear now generate a global token and
copy that and paste it in the field over
here the token is allowing Azure to
connect to Sona Cube and this will be
used for all pipeline runs that leverage
the same service connection for this
Serv connection name give it a
meaningful name because you'll be using
this later on inside your Azure develops
pipeline all right once you're done I
will want to Grant access permissions to
all pipelines in this case for demo sake
and I'll press the save button in my
Sona Cube instance press continue and
since my project is a JavaScript project
I will click on the JavaScript option
and I'll follow the instructions over
here normally you will have to create a
pipeline
and this will generate a Azure pipeline.
yo file so this is a note JS project and
you can see that over here I already
have one that I've created earlier on
I'll update the project key following
the instructions over here click on the
edit button and I'll update the project
key value over here next ensure that the
service connection name that you set
earli on is the same as the value over
here in this pipeline file so head over
to your settings service connections and
over here you see the the name of the
service connection is Joshua nodejs
service connection so update it to make
sure that this service connection is
correct press commit and commit the file
once the file is committed you can see
that your pipelines will begin to run
press continue and as you see the
pipeline runs you can just wait for Sona
Cube to be updated and there we go and
as you can see on the screen your
analysis scan is complete and showing on
SAA Cube and your pipeline on the left
has finished
running also take note of the new code
definition where the measures on new
code will appear only after the second
analysis of this
Branch next up we'll talk about polar
Crest analysis and polar Crest
decoration
so open up your Azure pipelines yo and
add a w card pattern for your future
branches and notice that I use a star
over here stop the pipeline from running
because whenever you commit it will
definitely trigger a pipeline run and so
I'm going to create a new branch and
over here this will actually trigger a
pipeline run which is the Baseline scan
for the branch so over here you can see
on the right side that I have my
Baseline scan for the branch next up you
will need to add build validation for
branches so head over to branches go
over to your main branch and note that
build validation has to be done on the
branch that you want to merge your pool
request into so go over here click on
Branch policies build validation and as
you see over here you will note that
ensure all your pull request get
automatically analyzed by adding a built
validation Branch policy on the target
Branch give it a meaningful name and
press the save button to save your built
policy configuration you might see an
Azure develops error but this could
possibly be an Azure develops bug ignore
this error and just press
save I'm going to create a file over
here I'm going to call it sample file
and after I commit the pipeline should
be triggered you can press the create a
pull request button over here and this
is where you will create a pull request
after that you will see that a pull
request pipeline job has been started
and once it's done you then be able to
see the pull request analysis that has
been run also you can see that there are
different icons for say the main branch
the pool request as well as the feature
Branch okay so this is the pool request
analysis and you can even click on the
assure devops icon to go into the
specific pull request and this is where
The annotation will be added on to the
poll request if you require any further
assistance we recommend that you go to
sonas source.com
ssupport where you can review your
support options including our free
community support as well as commercial
support if you've got a commercial
support contract with us and I'm sure
that our Sona sourcers will be happy to
help
[Music]

setup the user authentication project onboarding as well as the pool request analysis and decoration after logging in you land on the main Project screen where you can create a project select more go this config configuration a meaningful name and this will be what your whole configuration will saved as a URL also over here as you can see that I took from my Azure devops instance and as well as a personal access token click on user settings in Azure develops and go to personal access tokens where you'll be able to generate a new access token click on new token give it a name and this token will be used for all project integration if using the UI I'm going to set for 90 days take note that you have to give it the code read and write permissions so over here I'm going to select the read and write permissions and then create once I press create uh token generated I'm going to copy this and paste it inside my personal access token field press save configuration and this whole configuration will save paste the same token in and press list repositories and you'll be able to see your aure devops repositories loaded next we'll cover the authentication options available which
